news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels faces regulatory penalties from Rs. 557 to Rs. 4.07 cr.
Penalties include ESI delays, tax issues; company plans legal remedies.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels faces regulatory penalties from Rs. 557 to Rs. 4.07 cr.
Penalties include ESI delays, tax issues; company plans legal remedies.

news.title:
AI poses risks and opportunities for India's IT sector; pivoting to enterprise AI consulting may slow revenue growth.
GREED & Fear reshuffles portfolio: Home First Finance replaced by Adani Power; Ixigo swapped for IndiGo; Lemon Tree for Indian Hotels.
news.summary:
AI poses risks and opportunities for India's IT sector; pivoting to enterprise AI consulting may slow revenue growth.
GREED & Fear reshuffles portfolio: Home First Finance replaced by Adani Power; Ixigo swapped for IndiGo; Lemon Tree for Indian Hotels.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels reported its highest-ever Q3 FY26 revenue at Rs. 407.8 crore, growing 15% YoY, with EBITDA at Rs. 206.4 crore (up 12% YoY).
Renovation, technology investments, and GST changes impacted margins; RevPAR grew 9% YoY to Rs. 5,494. Pipeline includes 1,855 rooms with 259 hotels operational by Dec 2025.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels reported its highest-ever Q3 FY26 revenue at Rs. 407.8 crore, growing 15% YoY, with EBITDA at Rs. 206.4 crore (up 12% YoY).
Renovation, technology investments, and GST changes impacted margins; RevPAR grew 9% YoY to Rs. 5,494. Pipeline includes 1,855 rooms with 259 hotels operational by Dec 2025.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels posted record Q3 revenue of Rs. 407.8 Cr, up 15% YoY.
EBITDA reached Rs. 206.4 Cr but margin dipped 133 bps; PAT rose 2% YoY to Rs. 81.8 Cr.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels posted record Q3 revenue of Rs. 407.8 Cr, up 15% YoY.
EBITDA reached Rs. 206.4 Cr but margin dipped 133 bps; PAT rose 2% YoY to Rs. 81.8 Cr.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels' board to meet on Feb 6, 2026, to review Q3 and 9-month unaudited results for Dec 31, 2025.
Trading window for designated persons remains closed till 48 hours after the results' public announcement.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels' board to meet on Feb 6, 2026, to review Q3 and 9-month unaudited results for Dec 31, 2025.
Trading window for designated persons remains closed till 48 hours after the results' public announcement.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels announces Composite Scheme splitting into asset-light Lemon Tree and asset-heavy Fleur Hotels.
Warburg Pincus invests Rs. 960 cr in Fleur Hotels; Lemon Tree to be debt-free post-restructuring.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels announces Composite Scheme splitting into asset-light Lemon Tree and asset-heavy Fleur Hotels.
Warburg Pincus invests Rs. 960 cr in Fleur Hotels; Lemon Tree to be debt-free post-restructuring.

news.title: Warburg Pincus invests in Lemon Tree; Rs 1,300-1,400 Cr debt to transfer to Fleur.
news.summary: Warburg Pincus invests in Lemon Tree; Rs 1,300-1,400 Cr debt to transfer to Fleur.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd to host a conference call on Jan 15, 2026, at 4 PM IST for investors and analysts.
The call will discuss the Composite Scheme of Arrangement, led by senior management of Lemon Tree Hotels.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd to host a conference call on Jan 15, 2026, at 4 PM IST for investors and analysts.
The call will discuss the Composite Scheme of Arrangement, led by senior management of Lemon Tree Hotels.

news.title: Lemon Tree Hotels announces restructuring, value unlocking, and fund-raising plans.
news.summary: Lemon Tree Hotels announces restructuring, value unlocking, and fund-raising plans.

news.title:
Key stocks in focus: Bank of Baroda, Vedanta, Shriram Finance, Lemon Tree, Power Finance Corp due to strategic updates.
Major developments include Vedanta's demerger approval, Moody's upgraded outlook for Shriram Finance, and Lemon Tree's stake sale.
news.summary:
Key stocks in focus: Bank of Baroda, Vedanta, Shriram Finance, Lemon Tree, Power Finance Corp due to strategic updates.
Major developments include Vedanta's demerger approval, Moody's upgraded outlook for Shriram Finance, and Lemon Tree's stake sale.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels approves a scheme merging and demerging subsidiaries.
Demerged division turnover is ₹230.4 Cr, 60% of total revenue.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels approves a scheme merging and demerging subsidiaries.
Demerged division turnover is ₹230.4 Cr, 60% of total revenue.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels plans reorganization, creating two growth platforms.
Warburg Pincus acquires 41.1% stake in Fleur Hotels, committing ₹960 Cr for growth.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels plans reorganization, creating two growth platforms.
Warburg Pincus acquires 41.1% stake in Fleur Hotels, committing ₹960 Cr for growth.

news.title:
Warburg Pincus is expected to acquire a significant stake in Lemon Tree Hotels.
This reflects strong interest in the hospitality sector's post-pandemic recovery.
news.summary:
Warburg Pincus is expected to acquire a significant stake in Lemon Tree Hotels.
This reflects strong interest in the hospitality sector's post-pandemic recovery.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd to hold virtual meetings with institutional investors during Ambit's Investor Conference.
No UPSI or material information will be shared; discussions will use publicly available documents.
news.summary:

news.title:
Tata Power signed agreements for Bhutan's ₹13,100 crore hydropower project; Tata Chemicals announced ₹910 crore capacity expansions.
Natco Pharma faced FDA observations; RVNL, HG Infra won railway/metro bids; Lemon Tree expanded in Bhopal; Sandur secured MPAP allocation.
news.summary:
Tata Power signed agreements for Bhutan's ₹13,100 crore hydropower project; Tata Chemicals announced ₹910 crore capacity expansions.
Natco Pharma faced FDA observations; RVNL, HG Infra won railway/metro bids; Lemon Tree expanded in Bhopal; Sandur secured MPAP allocation.

news.title:
Lemon Tree Hotels reports highest Q2 revenue of Rs. 308 crore, up 8% YoY.
Net EBITDA rose 1% to Rs. 132.4 crore; PAT up 20% YoY at Rs. 41.9 crore.
news.summary:
Lemon Tree Hotels reports highest Q2 revenue of Rs. 308 crore, up 8% YoY.
